evals.name: metric-trend-with-tenant-scope
evals.prompt: hey, how many new listings did we get last month on the live marketplace? and roughly how does that compare to the month before — up or down?
evals.expected_output: Discovers the right schema/table via list_schemas (no guessing). Recognizes the marketplace data is multi-tenant and resolves which tenant the user means (asking if unclear) instead of blending tenants; scopes by passing the tenant argument, NOT by hand-writing a tenant_id filter. Returns two monthly counts with a clear up/down comparison, validates before running, and states the tenant, time window, and assumptions.

evals.name: cross-source-split-and-join
evals.prompt: can you work out average revenue per active user for last quarter? active = used the app in the period. i think users and payments live in different project DBs btw
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that users and payments are in different data sources, so it does NOT attempt a single cross-source join; instead runs one query per source (or explains why) and combines the results itself, defining 'active' explicitly and aggregating rather than dumping rows.

evals.name: dashboard-decomposition
evals.prompt: I want a little dashboard to keep an eye on marketplace health — signups over time, new listings per week, and which cities have the most completed transactions. can you set that up?
evals.expected_output: Decomposes into discrete widgets, picks sensible widget_type per widget (line/bar/metric), writes rolling-window queries (not hard-coded dates), previews results, and emits widget specs in the Report/Widget shape (title, widget_type, sql_query, config), while being clear that this MCP only reads and persistence is a separate step.

evals.name: access-gap-and-my-access
evals.prompt: what data do i actually have access to? and can i query the payments_live database — i need transaction amounts
evals.expected_output: Uses my_access to report the user's profiles and accessible schemas, checks payments_live specifically, and if it's not accessible, explains plainly that it's an access gap and how to request it from an admin — without inventing names or attempting to query it anyway.

evals.name: multi-tenant-disambiguation
evals.prompt: how many active listings do we have right now? for bayut
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the schema is multi-tenant and that 'bayut' is ambiguous (matches more than one tenant), so it asks the user which one / offers the options instead of guessing or blending tenants. Once a tenant is chosen, scopes the count to that single tenant via the tenant argument (never hand-writing tenant_id, never relying on the user to), and states which tenant the number is for. Never returns an all-tenant total as if it were one brand's, and only reads across tenants if the user explicitly asks (cross_tenant).
